name: Dummy Deck
description: Sandbox for testing new technical phrases before migration.

cards.sideA: Lo que quiero configurar es que esto haga eso.

sideB.text: What I want to set up is for this to do that.
What I want to configure is for this to do that.
What I am trying to set up is for it to do that.

cards.note:
**[er:194]**

~What I want to set up is this to do that~ and ~What I want to set up is to do that~ are not valid. When expressing that you want an object or system to perform an action, you must use the structure [for] + [object] + [to do]. The correct form is [for this to do that].

cards.sideA: Perdona, se me pego (congelo) el programa.

sideB.text: Sorry, my program froze.
My apologies, the program crashed on me.
Sorry, the program got stuck.
Sorry, the program got frozen.

cards.note:
**[er:218]**



~Sorry, the program got froze~ and ~Sorry, the program got freeze~ are not valid because the past participle of 'freeze' is [frozen]. You can say it [got frozen], but it is much more natural to just say it [froze].

cards.sideA: …Y ellos hacen su trabajo optimizando los binarios para generar las APPs finales.

sideB.text: ...And they do their job optimizing the binaries to generate the final apps.
...And they do their work optimizing the binaries to build the final apps.
...And they handle the optimization of the binaries to output the final apps.
And they work optimizing the binaries to output the final apps.

cards.sideA: Lo normal seria que mientras esta en produccion, le agregUemos esta feature.

sideB.text: Normally, while it is in production, we would add this feature.
The usual thing would be to add this feature while it's in production.
Ideally, we would add this feature while it is in production.
The normal way would be to add this feature while it is in production.

cards.note: ~The normal would be~ is a direct translation from Spanish. In English, use an adverb like [Normally] or add a noun like [The normal thing would be].

cards.sideA: Produccion en la base de datos suele cashear las busquedas y ser mas rapida.

sideB.text: The production database usually caches queries and is faster.
Production in the database tends to cache the searches and be faster.
The database in production usually caches the searches, making it faster.

cards.note:
~The database in production use to cache searching~ is not valid because 'use to' implies a past habit that no longer happens. For current routines, you should use [usually caches] or [tends to cache].

~Production in the database use to cache searching~ is incorrect because the subject is reversed. It should be the [database in production].

cards.sideA: Ellas pueden ser deployadas al mismo tiempo, no dependen del uno del otro.

sideB.text: They can be deployed at the same time, they don't depend on each other.
They can be deployed simultaneously, they are not interdependent.
They can be rolled out at the same time, they are independent of each other.

cards.sideA: Estoy afuera de la VPN, deja conectarme.

sideB.text: I'm off the VPN, let me connect.
I am not on the VPN, give me a second to connect.
I'm disconnected from the VPN, let me log in.

cards.note: ~out of the VPN~ is a direct translation; in English, we say we are [off] a network or VPN. Also, ~let me connect it~ is incorrect because you do not connect 'the VPN' itself, you connect yourself to it. Use [let me connect] or [let me log in].

cards.sideA: Podria causar problemas, por eso lo desactive.

sideB.text: It could cause problems, that's why I disabled it.
It might cause issues, so I turned it off.
It could lead to problems, which is why I deactivated it.
It could cause problems, therefore I disabled it.
It could cause problems, for that reason I deactivated it.

cards.note: ~desactivated~ is a spelling error influenced by Spanish. The correct English word is [deactivated].

cards.sideA: Desconozco sobre el ciclo de vida de una app en las stores.

sideB.text: I don't know about the lifecycle of an app in the stores.
I'm unfamiliar with the app lifecycle in the stores.
I am not aware of the lifecycle of an app on the stores.

cards.sideA: La familia se muda a una casa más grande en los suburbios.

sideB.text: The family moves to a larger house in the suburbs.
The family is moving to a bigger house in the suburbs.
The family relocates to a larger house in the suburbs.

cards.sideA: Está lento porque no están casheadas las queries en la base de dato.

sideB.text: It's slow because the queries are not cached in the database.
It is slow because the database queries aren't cached.
It's running slow because the queries haven't been cached in the database.
It's slow because the queries are not being cached in the database.

cards.sideA: Desde aqui nos podemos saltar las restricciones.

sideB.text: From here we can bypass the restrictions.
We can skip the restrictions from here.
From here, we can work around the restrictions.

cards.sideA: Desde aca podemos saltarnos las restricciones e ir directo por el resultado.

sideB.text: From here we can bypass the restrictions and go straight for the result.
We can skip the restrictions from here and get right to the outcome.
From here, we can bypass the constraints and go directly to the result.
From here we can bypass the restrictions and go direct for the result.
From here we can bypass the restrictions and go directly for the result.

cards.sideA: Lo acabo de pegar en el chat.

sideB.text: I just pasted it in the chat.
I've just dropped it in the chat.
I just shared it in the chat.

cards.sideA: Lo pegue en el chat hace un momento.

sideB.text: I pasted it in the chat a moment ago.
I shared it in the chat a while ago.
I just dropped it in the chat a few minutes ago.

cards.sideA: No he trabajado aun en ese issue, he priorizado otros.

sideB.text: I haven't worked on that issue yet, I've prioritized others.
I haven't tackled that issue yet, I've prioritized different ones.
I haven't gotten to that issue yet, I've put priority on others.

cards.sideA: He priorizado otros issues.

sideB.text: I've been prioritizing other issues.
I've put other issues first.
I've given priority to other issues.
I've prioritized other issues.

cards.sideA: Mi preocupación es que podria tardar mas de lo esperado.

sideB.text: My concern is that it could take longer than expected.
My worry is that it might take more time than anticipated.
What concerns me is that it might take longer than expected.
My concern is that they can be delayed longer than expected.

cards.sideA: Mi preocupación es que no lo hagamos a tiempo.

sideB.text: My concern is that we won't make it on time.
My worry is that we won't get it done in time.
I'm concerned that we won't finish it on time.

cards.note: ~My concern is to don't make it on time~ and ~My concern is don't make it on time~ are not valid because you cannot use the auxiliary 'don't' to negate an infinitive or as a noun clause. To express this, use a gerund phrase like [not making it] or a full clause like [that we won't make it]. ~My concern is to now make it on time~ is grammatically incorrect for expressing a negative; the correct infinitive negation is [not to make].

cards.sideA: Dame eso, pero bien hecho.

sideB.text: Give me that, but well done.
Give it to me, but make sure it's done right.
Give me that, but properly done.

cards.sideA: Damelo pero bien hecho.

sideB.text: Give it to me, but well done.
Pass it to me, but make sure it's high quality.
Give me that, but do it right.
Give me that, but well done.

cards.sideA: Las stores son realmente claras en comparacion con Oracle.

sideB.text: The stores are really clear compared to Oracle.
The stores are very straightforward compared to Oracle.
The stores are much easier to understand than Oracle.

cards.note: In this context, [compared to] functions as a prepositional phrase used to contrast two things. It originates from the past participle of the verb "compare", but here it simply acts as a fixed connector, similar to "like" or "against".

cards.sideA: Las Stores son mas bien claras en comparacion con EBS.

sideB.text: The stores are quite clear compared to EBS.
The stores are relatively clear in comparison with EBS.
The stores are fairly straightforward compared to EBS.

cards.note: In this context, [compared to] acts as a prepositional phrase used to contrast two things. Although "compared" originates from the past participle of the verb "compare", in this structure it functions together with "to" as a fixed connector, similar to "like" or "versus".

cards.sideA: ¿Está lloviendo? Si es así, lleva un paraguas.

sideB.text: Is it raining? If so, take an umbrella.
Is it raining? If that's the case, bring an umbrella.
Is it raining? If so, grab an umbrella.
Is it raining? If so, carry an umbrella.
Is it raining? If so, get an umbrella.

cards.sideA: ¿Viene él? De ser así, házmelo saber.

sideB.text: Is he coming? If so, let me know.
Is he showing up? If so, let me know.
Is he coming? If that's the case, let me know.

cards.sideA: ¿Funciona el enlace? En ese caso, haz clic en él.

sideB.text: Does the link work? If so, click it.
Is the link working? If so, click on it.
Does the link work? In that case, click it.

cards.note: ~Is it working the link? If so, click on it.~ is not valid because the pronoun 'it' and the noun 'the link' cannot be used together as the subject in this way. The correct question structure is [Is the link working?].

cards.sideA: ¿Es bajo el precio? De ser así, cómpralo ahora.

sideB.text: Is the price low? If so, buy it now.
Is it cheap? If so, buy it right now.
Is the price low? In that case, purchase it now.

cards.note: ~Is it low the price? If so, buy it now.~ is not valid because the word order is incorrect for a question; the verb 'is' must be followed directly by the subject, as in [Is the price low?].

cards.sideA: ¿Es esto aplicable para todas las provincias?

sideB.text: Is this applicable to all provinces?
Does this apply to all provinces?
Is this applicable across all provinces?
Is this applicable for all provinces?

cards.sideA: …pero para el otro dejarlo como ya es.

sideB.text: ...but for the other one, leave it as it is.
...but leave the other one the way it already is.
...but as for the other one, keep it the same.
...but for the other one, leave it how it is.

cards.sideA: Cuando la imagen de la red no carga, el banner usa el fallback.

sideB.text: When the network image doesn't load, the banner uses the fallback.
If the network image fails to load, the banner falls back to the default.
When the network image doesn't load, the banner relies on the fallback.

cards.note: ~When the img of the network doesn't load, the banner uses the fallback.~ is not valid because 'img of the network' is an unnatural translation; use [network image] or [image from the network].

cards.sideA: El fallback del banner es una imagen local guardada.

sideB.text: The banner's fallback is a locally stored image.
The banner's fallback is a saved local image.
The fallback for the banner is a locally saved image.
The fallback for the banner is a local saved image.
The fallback of the banner is a locally saved image.

cards.sideA: El banner carga desde la red, pero tiene un fallback como respaldo.

sideB.text: The banner loads from the network, but has a fallback as a backup.
The banner loads from the network but features a fallback backup.
The banner is loaded from the network, but it has a fallback just in case.
The banner loads from the internet, but has a fallback as a backup.

cards.note: ~The banner loads from the red, but has a fallback as a backup.~ is not valid because 'red' is a Spanish word; in English, use [network] or [internet].

cards.sideA: Tu puedes personalizar claude code como gustes.

sideB.text: You can customize Claude Code however you like.
You can customize Claude Code as you please.
You are able to customize Claude Code however you want.
You can customize Claude Code as you like.
You can personalize Claude Code however you like.

cards.sideA: La personalizacion es opcional en este caso.

sideB.text: Customization is optional in this case.
The customization is optional in this scenario.
Customizing it is optional in this case.

cards.sideA: Estoy afuera de la vpn, ¿me conecto?

sideB.text: I'm off the VPN, should I connect?
I am disconnected from the VPN, should I log in?
I'm not on the VPN, do you want me to connect?
I'm off the VPN, do I connect?
